---
title: "Networking in computational biology"
author: "Sean K. Maden"
date: "2023-08-07"
categories:
  - professional_networking
  - biotechnology
  - computational_biology_careers
  - job_networking_advice
---

Navigating and networking non-academic positions in computational biology can be exciting, envigorating, and uniquely challenging. These 
blog posts from several years back may come in handy for those who find themselves in a similar position.

* Title: Using the Informational Interview to Land a Job You'll Love
Date: May 18, 2015

<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metamaden/montyhall/master/plots/biotechblog1.png" alt="mh_ndoors_3plots" align="center" width=100%/>

* Title: Biotech Employers: Finding the Best Fit
Date: June 30, 2015

<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metamaden/montyhall/master/plots/biotechblog2.png" alt="mh_ndoors_3plots" align="center" width=100%/>

* Title: Decoding the Biotech Job Listing Part 1: "Good Communication", Collaboration, and Coevolution
Date: November 24, 2015

<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metamaden/montyhall/master/plots/biotechblog3.png" alt="mh_ndoors_3plots" align="center" width=100%/>
